task: Look at https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/issues/350 and suggest a fix

task: I use https://entire.io/ but it creates a large .entire directory, should this be git ignored? What does https://docs.entire.io/introduction say?
Is that what the entire documentation say? Why do I have this directory?
user_feedback_text: Is that what the entire documentation say? Why do I have this directory?

task: ok add the .entire directory, commit and push
Looking at entire documentation, anything I should put in entire settings?
Yes, I guess it is better to upgrade
user_feedback_text: Yes, I guess it is better to upgrade

task: merge main to this branch, commit and push
merge main to this branch, commit and push

Look at https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/pull/356 comments from greptile. Also thinking about it I think there are issues with your change, if someone has no auth and enables tailscale and bind, then the first user connecting could set auth. I think it would be best to have a "Access denied" when onboarding was already set, and let the user set auth either through terminal cli, or connecting the the interface via localhost.
user_feedback_text: Look at https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/pull/356 comments from greptile. Also thinking about it I think there are issues with your change, if someone has no auth and enables tailscale and bind, then the first user connecting could set auth. I think it would be best to have a "Access denied" when onboarding was already set, and let the user set auth either through terminal cli, or connecting the the interface via localhost.
